chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 110 of 2018 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 11.05.2018 Mrs. Mehmooda Sulleman, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, Petitioner Surosh Ahmed Wasif has prayed for the grant of Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration in respect of moveable and immoveable properties/assets, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Properties attached with the memo of petition, available at page 21 & 23, which were left by his deceased parents, namely, Ahmed Inamul Hai Wasif s/o. Late Muhammad Abdul Hai and Safia Sultana widow of Ahmed Inamul Hai Wasif, who died on 10.04.2015 and 10.02.2017, respectively at Karachi, leaving behind (i) Surosh Ahmed Wasif (petitioner), (ii) Adeel Ahmed Kashif and (iii) Madiha Omar as their legal heirs. Original Death Certificates of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA, title documents of the immovable properties and bank statements are also filed at page 15 to 19 and 33 to 47. The legal heir No. 3 has filed her Affidavit of No-Objection for the grant of Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner, while petitioner has filed Affidavit of No- Objection on behalf of legal heir No. 2, who has executed General Power of Attorney duly attested by the Third Secretary/AAO in Consulate General of Pakistan at Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, which is available in the case file. Two witnesses, namely, Syed Ahmed Shoaib and Syed Ahmed Zubair both sons of Makhdoom Ali have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above.

The legal heir No. 3 has filed her Affidavit of No-Objection for the grant of Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner, while petitioner has filed Affidavit of No- Objection on behalf of legal heir No. 2, who has executed General Power of Attorney duly attested by the Third Secretary/AAO in Consulate General of Pakistan at Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, which is available in the case file. Two witnesses, namely, Syed Ahmed Shoaib and Syed Ahmed Zubair both sons of Makhdoom Ali have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 16.03.2018; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 26.04.2018. Today legal heir No.3 and above named witnesses are present and affirm the contents of their affidavits and also verify their signatures thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration in respect of movable and immovable properties/assets shown in the Schedule of Properties, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
